problem.title: Paint House
problem.description: There are n houses in a row, each of which can be painted with one of three colors: red (0), blue (1), or green (2). The cost of painting house i with color j is costs[i][j]. No two adjacent houses can have the same color. Find the minimum total cost to paint all houses.
assumptions: Adjacent houses must have different colors.

example.explanation: Paint house 0 blue (2), house 1 green (5), house 2 blue (3). Total = 10.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Define dp[i][c] as the minimum cost to paint houses 0 through i such that house i is painted color c. The recurrence is dp[i][c] = costs[i][c] + min(dp[i-1][c'] for c' != c). Since there are only 3 colors, for each color we take the minimum over the other two, giving a constant-time update per cell. We can update in-place on the costs array to use O(1) extra space.

complexity.time: O(n) — one pass through n houses with constant work per house
complexity.space: O(1) — in-place update on costs array
